description: A bump chart: rank (a table calc of an aggregated measure) plotted over a Year/Month date on cols, one Line per category (color), with a Circle dot and rank label at each period — shows how members' rankings rise and fall across time. Lane W11-F2: the source year pin was PARAMETERIZED (collapsed to level-members) so the date stays a data-driven Filters pill with no source literals.

slots.notes: Year part of the SAME bound date field (outer cols axis). Reuses Order Date at a second derivation — see hazard single-date-two-derivations.
Month part of the SAME bound date field (inner cols axis) — the period the rank is recomputed at.
One ranked line/color per member — the entities whose ranks bump over time.

The measure that is ranked. It is not on a shelf directly; it feeds the template-owned Rank(Sum([Sales])) table calc (see hazard table-calc-rank-rides-along). replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Sales] inside that formula on bind.

hazards.detail: The source workbook pinned Year of Order Date to member='2016' — a source-calendar literal that would render empty on a differently-shaped date. Lane F2's compile-time filter policy PARAMETERIZED it: the member pin was collapsed to the neutral all-members form (<groupfilter function='level-members' level='[yr:Order Date:ok]'/>), so Year stays a data-driven Filters-shelf pill (its <slices> entry preserved) with the source literal removed — the same neutral form the apply-path 3c neutralizer writes (idempotent).

The one [Order Date] field is used at BOTH Year and Month on cols. Against the single-date committed fixture the second temporal slot cannot bind a DISTINCT field, so fixture_bind=false — a valid, honest state (same as change-over-time-calendar-heatmap). On a real dataset both slots bind the same date field via qualified keys; the false reflects the fixture's one-date shape, not a template defect.

The Rank measure [Calculation_508343847073411072] is a table calc 'Rank(Sum([Sales]))' with a nested <table-calc>. Its <calculation> is NON-self-closing, so parseTemplateCalcs (self-closing only) does NOT see it — it is NOT a declared calc slot and rides along verbatim. Its one bindable input is Sales (the 'sales' slot); replaceFieldReferences rewrites [Sales] inside the formula on bind. The ranking assumes the bound measure is meaningfully rankable across the categories.
